LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRANSDEV SERVICES, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTIONS 17200 AND 17500. PLAINTIFF IS NOT A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE CONTRACT BETWEEN THE CITY AND DEFENDANT. THE CONTRACT DOES NOT DISCLOSE AN INTENT TO BENEFIT PLAINTIFF OR ANY OTHER MEMBER OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC WHO WAS NOT A RECIPIENT OF PARATRANSIT SERVICES. ANY BENEFIT THAT PLAINTIFF OR THE GENERAL PUBLIC RECEIVED FROM THE CONTRACT WAS INCIDENTAL AND THUS INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY STANDING. THE 17200 AND 17500 CLAIMS DO NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE ECONOMIC INJURY BY PLAINTIFF, A SPECIFIC MISREPRESENTATION OR INSTANCE OF FALSE ADVERTISING BY DEFENDANT OR RELIANCE BY PLAINTIFF. BECAUSE IT IS NOT CLEAR WHETHER PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO REMEDY THE DEFICIENCIES OF THE 17200 AND 17500 CLAIMS, HE IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE COGNIZABLE CLAIMS FOR VIOLATIONS OF SECTIONS 17200 AND 17500, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF ALL SIX EXHIBITS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF CERTIFICATE OF INSURANCE IS SUSTAINED, AND DEFENDANT'S REQUST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF CERTIFICATE OF INSURANCE IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Nov-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Joseph P. Brent of Brent & Fiol, Tel. No. 1-415-839-8370 ext. 4
appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Jesse C. Medlong & Atty. David F. Gross of DLA Piper, Tel. No. 1-415-836-2594
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Transdev Services, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings on the second cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code 17200 is granted with thirty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Ralph Oliverio's request to hold this motion in abeyance until he files a motion for reconsideration is denied. This motion is ripe for resolution now based on the complaint filed by Mr. Oliverio and there is no persuasive explanation why an unfiled motion will have any effect on the sole issue on this motion whether the second cause of action states a valid UCL claim based on the unlawful prong. Because they are referenced and made mandatory in SF Transportation Code 1106(j), SFMTA minimum insurance requirements constitute a law for purposes of the unlawful prong of the UCL. However, Mr. Oliverio's UCL claim fails because he has not identified any law, including the SFMTA minimum insurance requirements, that imposes an obligation on Transdev that Transdev allegedly violated. To the extent Mr. Oliverio contends that Transdev is liable on conspiracy or aiding and abetting theories, Mr. Oliverio does not allege facts showing that Transdev owed a duty to him or what Transdev did that constitutes either conspiring or aiding and abetting Yellow Cab in violating the minimum insurance requirements. (Applied Equip. Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 511(tort liability arising from conspiracy presupposes that the coconspirator owes a duty to the plaintiff recognized by law)). If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Oliverio has leave to remedy these defects and also to allege a negligence cause of action against Transdev. That if the case is dismissed as to current defendant, the two arbitration consolidated will return to arbitration and not on this case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Continued To Aug-01-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Transdev Services Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Overruling Defendant'S Demurrer As To Plaintiff'S Unfair Competition Claim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar May-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. David Fiol of Brant & Fiol, Tel. No. 1-415-259-4420 appearing for the
Plaintiff;
Atty. Jesse C. Medlong & Atty. David Gross of DLA Piper LLP. Tel. No. 1-415-636-2594
appearing for the Defendant.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Transdev Services, Inc.'s "motion for an order overruling demurrer to plaintiff's unfair competition claim" is DENIED. Transdev's demurrer has already been overruled by the Court of Appeal's December 22, 2017 decision, which called for no further demurrer order by the trial court, but simply "further proceedings." (See, e.g., Aroney v. California Horse Racing Board (1983) 145 Cal.App.3d 928, 933 (on remand, trial court instructed to "vacate its order sustaining the demurrer and to enter a new and different order overruling the demurrer").) Transdev is ordered to answer the complaint on or before June 4, 2018. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-04-2016 Continued To Nov-16-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JOSEPH P. BRENT, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-984-0415) and DAVID L. FIOL, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-259-4420) of Brent, Fiol & Pratt LLP appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. ALEC CIERNY, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-615-6016) and DAVID F. GROSS, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-836-2562) of DLA Piper appearing for Defendant Transdev Services, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Transdev Services, Inc's demurrer to complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause for breach of contract and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for violations of Business and Professions Code sections 17200 and 17500. Plaintiff is not a third party beneficiary of the contract between the City and defendant. The contract does not disclose an intent to benefit plaintiff or any other member of the general public who was not a recipient of paratransit services. Any benefit that plaintiff or the general public received from the contract was incidental and thus insufficient to support third party beneficiary standing. The 17200 and 17500 claims do not adequately allege economic injury by plaintiff, a specific misrepresentation or instance of false advertising by defendant or reliance by plaintiff. Because it is not clear whether plaintiff may be able to remedy the deficiencies of the 17200 and 17500 claims, he is granted leave to allege cognizable claims for violations of sections 17200 and 17500, if he can do so in good faith. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice of all six exhibits is granted. Plaintiff's objection to Defendant's request for judicial notice of certificate of insurance is sustained, and Defendant's requst for judicial notice of certificate of insurance is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
